---
title: "Untitled"
format: html
editor: visual
---

Simulate data for Linear quantile regression

Heteroskedastic error with increasing variance simulation

OLS

```{r eval = T, warning = F, message = F}
# Library section
library(quantreg)
library(ggplot2)
```

```{r eval = T, warning = F, message = F}
set.seed(479)
sample_size <- c(50, 100, 200, 400, 600)
repli <- 1000
```

```{r eval = T, warning = F, message = F}
set.seed(479)
# List of needed metrics
coverage <- numeric(length(sample_size)) #set coverage with the same obs as sample size
n_sizes <- length(sample_size)
mean_slope <- numeric(n_sizes)
sd_slope <- numeric(n_sizes)
mean_se_slope  <- numeric(n_sizes)
power <- numeric(n_sizes)

# Slope by bootstrap
slope_func <- function(data, indices) {
  d <- data[indices, ]
  fit <- lm(y ~ x, data = d)
  coef(fit)[2]
}

# Looping areaaaaaaaa
for (i in seq_along(sample_size)) {
  chosen_size <- sample_size[i]
  
  coverage.estimate <- logical(repli)
  slope <- numeric(repli)
  se_slope <- numeric(repli)
  error <- numeric(repli)
  beta1.true <- 0.4
  power_count <- logical(repli)

  for (j in 1:repli) {
    # data set creation
    x <- runif(chosen_size, min = 0, max = 5)
    y <- y <- 20 + 0.4 * x + rnorm(chosen_size, 0, 3.5) * (1 + x/7)
    dat <- data.frame(x = x, y = y)
    
    # OLS
    ols <- lm(y ~ x, data = dat)
    slope[j] <- coef(ols)[2]
    se_slope[j] <- summary(ols)$coefficients["x", "Std. Error"]
    
    # Build 95% confidence interval
    ci_lower <- slope[j] - 1.96 * se_slope[j]
    ci_upper <- slope[j] + 1.96 * se_slope[j]
    
    coverage.estimate[j] <- (ci_lower <= beta1.true) && (ci_upper >= beta1.true)
    power_count[j] <- (summary(ols)$coefficients["x", "Pr(>|t|)"] < 0.05)
    
  }
  
  coverage[i] <- mean(coverage.estimate) * 100
  mean_slope[i] <- mean(slope)
  mean_se_slope[i] <- mean(se_slope)
  sd_slope[i] <- sd(slope)
  power[i] <- mean(power_count)*100
}
results <- data.frame(
  n              = sample_size,
  Mean_Slope     = round(mean_slope, 4),
  SE_Slope       = round(mean_se_slope, 4),
  Coverage_pct   = round(coverage, 1),
  POWER = round(power, 1)
)

print("OLS")
print(results)
```

```{r eval = T, warning = F, message = F}
set.seed(479)

beta_original <- 0.4
eps_sig <- 3.5
b <- 1/7
taus <- c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 0.9)

n_sizes <- length(sample_size)
results <- list()

for (tau in taus) {
  cat("Quantile =", tau, "\n")
  beta1.true <- beta_original + qnorm(tau) * eps_sig * b
  cat("True slope =", beta1.true, "\n")
  
  n_sizes <- length(sample_size)
  
  coverage <- numeric(n_sizes)
  power <- numeric(n_sizes)
  mean_slope <- numeric(n_sizes)
  sd_slope <- numeric(n_sizes)
  mean_se_slope <- numeric(n_sizes)
  qloss <- numeric(n_sizes)
  
  for (i in seq_along(sample_size)) {
    n <- sample_size[i]
  
    coverage.estimate <- logical(repli)
    slope <- numeric(repli)
    se_slope <- numeric(repli)
    error <- numeric(repli)
    power_count <- logical(repli)
    
    for (j in 1:repli) {
      x <- runif(n, min = 0, max = 5)
      y <- 20 + 0.4 * x + rnorm(n, 0, 3.5) * (1 + x / 7)
      
      dat <- data.frame(x = x, y = y)
      qr <- rq(y ~ x, tau = tau, data = dat)
      
      slope[j] <- coef(qr)[2]
      
      # Fixed: Use "nid" instead of "iid" (better for heteroskedasticity)
      sum_rq <- summary(qr, se = "nid")
      se_slope[j] <- sum_rq$coefficients["x", "Std. Error"]
     
      ci_lower <- slope[j] - 1.96 * se_slope[j]
      ci_upper <- slope[j] + 1.96 * se_slope[j]
      
      coverage.estimate[j] <- (ci_lower <= beta1.true) && (ci_upper >= beta1.true)
      error[j] <- mean(abs(dat$y - predict(qr, newdata = dat)))
      power_count[j] <- sum_rq$coefficients["x", "Pr(>|t|)"] < 0.05
    }
    
    coverage[i] <- mean(coverage.estimate, na.rm = TRUE) * 100
    mean_slope[i] <- mean(slope, na.rm = TRUE)
    mean_se_slope[i] <- mean(se_slope, na.rm = TRUE)
    sd_slope[i] <- sd(slope, na.rm = TRUE)
    qloss[i] <- mean(error, na.rm = TRUE)
    power[i] <- mean(power_count, na.rm = TRUE) * 100
  }
  
  results_tau <- data.frame(
    n = sample_size,
    Mean_Slope = round(mean_slope, 4),
    SD_Slope = round(sd_slope, 4),
    SE_Slope = round(mean_se_slope, 4),
    Quantile_Loss = round(qloss, 4),
    Coverage_pct = round(coverage, 1),
    Power_pct = round(power, 1)
  )
  
  results[[as.character(tau)]] <- results_tau
  print(results_tau)
  cat("\n")
}
```

Skewed with constant variance simulation

OLS

```{r eval = T, warning = F, message = F}
# library(sn) # library of skew to normal dsitribution
set.seed(479)

skewness <- c(0, 1, 2, 3, 6)
beta1_original <- 0.4
beta0_original <- 20

sample_length <- length(sample_size)

for (skew in skewness) {
  cat("\nSkewness =", skew, "\n")
 
  avg_slope          <- numeric(sample_length)
  avg_bias_percent   <- numeric(sample_length)
  coverage_percent   <- numeric(sample_length)
  avg_interval_length<- numeric(sample_length)
  power_percent      <- numeric(sample_length)
  mae                <- numeric(sample_length)
  
  for (i in seq_along(sample_size)) {
    n <- sample_size[i]
    
    slope            <- numeric(repli)
    bias             <- numeric(repli)
    coverage.estimate<- logical(repli)
    interval_length  <- numeric(repli)
    power            <- logical(repli)
    absolute_bias    <- numeric(repli)
    se_slope         <- numeric(repli)
   
    for (j in 1:repli) {
      # Generate error
      if (skew == 0) {
        error <- rnorm(n, 0, 3.5)
      } else {
        shape <- 4 / skew^2
        rate  <- sqrt(shape) / 3.5
        error <- rgamma(n, shape = shape, rate = rate)
        error <- error - shape / rate   # center to mean 0
      }
     
      x <- runif(n, 0, 5)
      y <- beta0_original + beta1_original * x + error
      dat <- data.frame(x = x, y = y)
     
      ols <- lm(y ~ x, data = dat)
      slope[j] <- coef(ols)[2]
      
      # Extract SE
      se_slope[j] <- summary(ols)$coefficients["x", "Std. Error"]
      
      # Build 95% CI (fixed)
      ci_lower <- slope[j] - 1.96 * se_slope[j]
      ci_upper <- slope[j] + 1.96 * se_slope[j]
      
      # Coverage (fixed index)
      coverage.estimate[j] <- (ci_lower <= beta1_original) && (ci_upper >= beta1_original)
      
      bias[j]          <- (slope[j] - beta1_original) / beta1_original * 100
      interval_length[j] <- ci_upper - ci_lower
      power[j]         <- summary(ols)$coefficients["x", "Pr(>|t|)"] < 0.05
      
      absolute_bias[j] <- abs(slope[j] - beta1_original) / beta1_original * 100
    }
    
    # Summarize
    avg_slope[i]           <- mean(slope)
    avg_bias_percent[i]    <- mean(bias)
    coverage_percent[i]    <- mean(coverage.estimate) * 100
    avg_interval_length[i] <- mean(interval_length)
    power_percent[i]       <- mean(power) * 100
    mae[i]                 <- mean(absolute_bias)
  }
  
  results <- data.frame(
    n                  = sample_size,
    avg_slope          = round(avg_slope, 4),
    bias_percent       = round(avg_bias_percent, 4),
    avg_interval_leng  = round(avg_interval_length, 4),
    coverage_per       = round(coverage_percent, 2),   # changed to 2 decimals usually enough
    power              = round(power_percent, 2),
    mae                = round(mae, 4)
  )
  
  print("OLS under skewness")
  print(results)
}
```

```{r eval = T, warning = F, message = F}
typeI_results <- list()
for (skew in skewness) {
  cat("\nSkewness =", skew, "\n")
  
  typeI_rate        <- numeric(length(sample_size))   # currently unused
  avg_slope         <- numeric(sample_length)
  avg_bias_percent  <- numeric(sample_length)
  coverage_percent  <- numeric(sample_length)
  avg_interval_length <- numeric(sample_length)
  power_percent     <- numeric(sample_length)
  mae               <- numeric(sample_length)
  
  for (i in seq_along(sample_size)) {
    n <- sample_size[i]
    
    slope          <- numeric(repli)
    bias           <- numeric(repli)
    coverage       <- logical(repli)
    interval_length<- numeric(repli)
    power          <- logical(repli)
    absolute_bias  <- numeric(repli)
    
    for (j in 1:repli) {
      # Error generation
      if (skew == 0) {
        error <- rnorm(n, 0, 3.5)
      } else {
        shape <- 4 / skew^2
        rate  <- sqrt(shape) / 3.5
        error <- rgamma(n, shape = shape, rate = rate)
        error <- error - shape / rate   # center to mean 0
      }
      
      x <- runif(n, 0, 5)
      y <- beta0_original + beta1_original * x + error
      dat <- data.frame(x = x, y = y)
      
      # Fit median quantile regression (tau = 0.5)
      qr_fit <- rq(y ~ x, tau = 0.5, data = dat)
      
      slope[j] <- coef(qr_fit)[2]
      bias[j]  <- (slope[j] - beta1_original) / beta1_original * 100
      absolute_bias[j] <- abs(slope[j] - beta1_original) / beta1_original * 100
      
      # === Normal approximation CI (no bootstrap) ===
      sum_qr <- summary(qr_fit, se = "nid")           # "nid" is recommended
      se_slope <- sum_qr$coefficients["x", "Std. Error"]
      
      ci_lower <- slope[j] - 1.96 * se_slope
      ci_upper <- slope[j] + 1.96 * se_slope
      
      coverage[j]       <- (ci_lower <= beta1_original) && (ci_upper >= beta1_original)
      interval_length[j]<- ci_upper - ci_lower
      power[j]          <- !(ci_lower <= 0 && ci_upper >= 0)
    }
    
    # Summarize across replications
    avg_slope[i]           <- mean(slope)
    avg_bias_percent[i]    <- mean(bias)
    coverage_percent[i]    <- mean(coverage) * 100
    avg_interval_length[i] <- mean(interval_length)
    power_percent[i]       <- mean(power) * 100
    mae[i]                 <- mean(absolute_bias)
  }
  
  results <- data.frame(
    n                 = sample_size,
    avg_slope         = round(avg_slope, 4),
    bias_percent      = round(avg_bias_percent, 4),
    avg_interval_leng = round(avg_interval_length, 4),
    coverage_per      = round(coverage_percent, 2),
    power             = round(power_percent, 2),
    mae               = round(mae, 4)
  )
  
  print("QR (Median, Normal Approx CI)")
  print(results)
}
```

```{r}

```
